title: PhysiX: A Foundation Model for Physics Simulations
abstract: We introduce PhysiX, a family of large-scale foundation models for physics simulation. PhysiX is an autoregressive generative model composed of a discrete tokenizer, which converts heterogeneous physical processes at different scales into a sequence of discrete tokens, and employs an autoregressive next-token prediction objective to model such processes.
method_summary: 4.5B-parameter autoregressive foundation model that tokenizes heterogeneous physics simulations (fluids, solids, multi-physics) into discrete tokens and uses next-token prediction, achieving cross-domain generalization without task-specific fine-tuning.

title: OmniArch: Building Foundation Model for Scientific Computing
abstract: Foundation models have revolutionized language modeling, while whether this success is replicated in scientific computing remains unexplored. We present OmniArch, the first prototype aiming at solving diverse PDEs across different physics domains with a single unified model.
method_summary: First unified foundation model for multi-physics PDE solving, using an axis-based encoder and decoder-only Transformer to handle diverse scientific computing tasks (heat, fluid, elasticity) in a single architecture.

title: Unified Physics Transformers: A Framework For Efficiently Scaling Neural Operators
abstract: With ever increasing problem complexity, the natural question arises: what is an efficient way to scale neural operators to larger and more complex simulations. We propose Unified Physics Transformers (UPT) that encode data flexibly into a compressed latent space representation, allowing efficient operation without relying on grid or particle-based latent spaces.
method_summary: Proposes Unified Physics Transformers that compress heterogeneous physics data (grids, particles, meshes) into a unified latent space, enabling efficient scaling of neural operators to larger simulations.

title: Towards a Physics Foundation Model
abstract: Access to a Physics Foundation Model (PFM) would be transformative -- democratizing access to high-fidelity simulations, accelerating scientific discovery, and eliminating the need for specialized solvers. We propose a roadmap toward building such a model, identifying key challenges in data, architecture, and evaluation.
method_summary: Proposes a roadmap for Physics Foundation Models, analyzing architectural choices (tokenization, attention, conditioning), data requirements, and evaluation protocols for cross-domain physics simulation.

title: PINNsAgent: Automated PDE Surrogation with Large Language Models
abstract: Solving partial differential equations (PDEs) using neural methods has been a long-standing pursuit. Physics-Informed Neural Networks (PINNs) have emerged as a promising approach but require extensive manual tuning. We introduce PINNsAgent, a novel surrogation framework that leverages LLMs to automate PINN parameter tuning and architecture selection.
method_summary: Uses LLM agents to automate the configuration and tuning of PINNs for PDE solving, bridging domain-specific knowledge with deep learning through natural language interaction and automated hyperparameter search.

title: Physics-Informed Diffusion Models
abstract: We present a framework that unifies generative modeling and partial differential equation fulfillment by introducing a first-principle-based loss term that enforces generated samples to fulfill the underlying PDE constraints during the diffusion process.
method_summary: Unifies diffusion models with physics-informed constraints by embedding PDE residual losses into the denoising process, significantly improving physical consistency of generated samples for engineering simulation.

title: Multi-scale Topology Optimization using Neural Networks
abstract: We propose a new method for multi-scale topology optimization that seamlessly ensures compatibility across neighboring microstructure cells without the need for a catalog of unit cells and associated connectivity constraints. Our approach consists of a topology neural network that optimizes the microstructure shape and distribution across the design domain as a continuous field.
method_summary: Neural network-based multi-scale topology optimization that generates spatially varying microstructures as a continuous field, ensuring inter-cell compatibility without pre-computed unit cell catalogs. Published at SIGGRAPH.

title: Consistent Machine Learning for Topology Optimization with Microstructure-Dependent Neural Network Material Models
abstract: Additive manufacturing methods together with topology optimization have enabled the creation of multiscale structures with controlled spatially-varying material microstructure. This work presents a framework for designing multiscale heterogeneous structures by merging homogenization-based topology optimization with consistent neural network material models that enforce polyconvexity, objectivity, and thermodynamic consistency.
method_summary: Merges homogenization-based topology optimization with physics-consistent neural network material models (enforcing polyconvexity, objectivity, symmetry) for multiscale structure design with spatially varying microstructures.

title: Guiding Topology Optimization Diffusion with Human Preferences
abstract: Inspired by preference alignment techniques in generative modeling, our method learns to suppress unrealistic outputs by modulating the reverse diffusion trajectory using gradients of human-aligned reward models for topology optimization.
method_summary: Applies RLHF-style preference alignment to diffusion-based topology optimization, using human preference reward models to guide the generative process toward designs that match engineer expectations.

title: Agentic LLM Orchestration of Engineering Analysis in Product Development Design Practice
abstract: This paper presents an agentic LLM framework that orchestrates engineering analysis workflows in product development, including FEA setup, mesh generation, solver configuration, and result interpretation, through natural language interaction with CAE tools.
method_summary: Proposes an agentic LLM system that orchestrates end-to-end engineering analysis (FEA/CFD) in product development, automating CAE workflow from problem specification to result interpretation via natural language.

title: SimuAgent: An LLM-Based Simulink Modeling Assistant Enhanced with Reinforcement Learning
abstract: We introduce SimuAgent, an LLM-powered modeling and simulation agent tailored for Simulink. SimuAgent replaces verbose XML with a concise, dictionary-style Python representation, dramatically cutting context length. Trained with reflection-guided reinforcement learning, it plans and executes modeling tasks with high accuracy.
method_summary: LLM agent for Simulink modeling that uses compact Python representations and reflection-guided RL training to automate block diagram construction, parameter tuning, and simulation execution.

title: GRACE: Efficient and Feasible Robotic Assembly Sequence Planning via Graph Representation Learning
abstract: Automatic Robotic Assembly Sequence Planning (RASP) can significantly improve productivity and resilience in modern manufacturing. With GRACE, we extract meaningful information from graph input and predict assembly sequences in a step-by-step manner using graph attention networks.
method_summary: Uses graph attention networks for robotic assembly sequence planning, learning to predict feasible assembly orders from part relationship graphs with physical constraint satisfaction.

title: Deep Neural Implicit Representation of Accessibility for Multi-Axis Manufacturing
abstract: One of the main concerns in design and process planning for multi-axis additive and subtractive manufacturing is collision avoidance between moving objects and stationary objects. We develop an implicit representation of the collision measure field via deep neural networks that can accurately interpolate the collision measure for arbitrary tool orientations.
method_summary: Learns implicit neural representations of manufacturing accessibility/collision fields for multi-axis machining, enabling rapid collision checking for arbitrary tool orientations without explicit geometric computation.

title: DeepMill: Neural Accessibility Learning for Subtractive Manufacturing
abstract: We introduce DeepMill, the first neural framework designed to accurately and efficiently predict inaccessible and occlusion regions under varying machining tool parameters, applicable to both 3-axis and 5-axis CNC machining configurations.
method_summary: First neural framework for predicting machining accessibility and occlusion regions for 3-axis and 5-axis CNC, replacing expensive geometric computation with learned predictions that generalize across tool parameters.

title: Implicit Neural Field-Based Process Planning for Multi-Axis Manufacturing
abstract: We present an implicit neural field-based framework for multi-axis process planning that overcomes limitations of discrete layer-based approaches by embedding both layer generation and toolpath design within a single differentiable implicit field representation using sinusoidally activated neural networks.
method_summary: Embeds multi-axis manufacturing process planning (layer generation + toolpath design) into a single differentiable implicit neural field, enabling continuous optimization of the entire manufacturing plan.

title: Large Language Model-Empowered Next-Generation Computer-Aided Engineering
abstract: Software development has entered a new era where large language models now serve as general-purpose reasoning engines, enabling natural language interaction and transformative applications across engineering domains. This paper surveys how LLMs are reshaping computer-aided engineering including FEA automation, CFD workflow orchestration, and design optimization.
method_summary: Surveys the emerging paradigm of LLM-empowered CAE, covering automated FEA/CFD setup, natural language-driven simulation, intelligent post-processing, and the path toward autonomous engineering analysis agents.

title: Automating Data-Driven Modeling and Analysis for Engineering Applications using Large Language Model Agents
abstract: In this study, we propose an innovative pipeline utilizing Large Language Model agents to automate data-driven modeling and analysis, with emphasis on regression tasks for engineering applications including surrogate modeling and material property prediction.
method_summary: Proposes an LLM agent pipeline that automates the entire data-driven modeling workflow for engineering (data preprocessing, feature selection, model training, validation) through natural language instructions.

title: Fast and Accurate Bayesian Optimization with Pre-trained Transformers for Constrained Engineering Problems
abstract: Bayesian Optimization (BO) is a foundational strategy for efficiently handling black-box functions with many constraints and expensive evaluations. We propose using pre-trained transformer models as surrogate functions within BO, achieving faster convergence on constrained engineering design problems.
method_summary: Replaces traditional Gaussian process surrogates in Bayesian optimization with pre-trained transformers, achieving faster convergence on constrained engineering design problems with expensive evaluations.

title: Large Language Models for Combinatorial Optimization of Design Structure Matrix
abstract: In complex engineering systems, the dependencies between components create intricate coupling patterns that significantly impact development efficiency. We apply LLMs to optimize Design Structure Matrices, a fundamental tool in systems engineering for managing component interdependencies.
method_summary: Applies LLMs to combinatorial optimization of Design Structure Matrices in systems engineering, using natural language reasoning to reorder and cluster coupled engineering components for efficient development.

title: Connector-Aware General Robotic Assembly from Instruction Manuals via Vision-Language Models
abstract: Assembly hinges on reliably forming connections between parts; yet most robotic approaches plan assembly sequences and part poses while treating connectors as an afterthought. We propose a connector-aware assembly framework that uses vision-language models to parse instruction manuals and reason about connector types and mating constraints.
method_summary: Uses vision-language models to parse assembly instruction manuals and reason about connector types, enabling connector-aware robotic assembly planning that explicitly models mating constraints.

title: AssemMate: Graph-Based LLM for Robotic Assembly Assistance
abstract: This graph-based LLM enables knowledge graph question answering (KGQA), supporting human-robot interaction and assembly task planning for specific products. Beyond interactive QA, AssemMate also supports automated assembly sequence generation from product knowledge graphs.
method_summary: Combines knowledge graphs with LLMs for robotic assembly assistance, enabling natural language interaction for assembly planning and automated sequence generation from product structure knowledge.
